title: NOC 11103 – Securities Agents, Investment Dealers and Brokers | GTR Immigration
description: Securities Agents, Investment Dealers and Brokers under NOC 11103 are highly valued professionals in Canada’s financial services and immigration landscape. These experts manage securities trading, investment portfolios, and brokerage services—making them strong candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Professionals in this occupation may perform some or all of the following:
Develop client relationships and design investment strategies
Monitor market data and industry publications
Execute trades in stocks, bonds, treasury bills, mutual funds, and derivatives
Ensure regulatory compliance for all transactions
Trade securities and commodities on exchange floors
Develop trading strategies based on market intelligence
Prepare transaction records and trade tickets
Analyze portfolio positions and market movements
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in economics, business, or related fields
Recognized designations such as CFA, CFP, CIM, or equivalents
Completion of Canadian Securities Course and Conduct & Practices Handbook Course (or approved alternatives)
Provincial securities licensing or registration with recognized regulatory bodies
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 6,700 new jobs for Securities Agents, Investment Dealers and Brokers, while approximately 5,800 job seekers may enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—especially applicants from India—this presents favorable opportunities with the right immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Securities Agents, Investment Dealers and Brokers remain in demand across Canada’s stock exchanges, brokerage houses, and investment firms. Post-pandemic market expansion and increased investor participation continue to strengthen hiring outlooks.
Favorable immigration opportunities for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $48,200 to $161,900, depending on experience, region, and specialization.
Strong labour standards and work-life balance
Direct pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
